summary: Resolve Q-0001 by deliberately permitting the OSI-approved permissive Unicode-3.0 license used transitively by ICU4X and unicode-ident, with assertion-level parser coverage and retained all-green Rust and combined dependency-policy evidence.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Add Unicode-3.0 to ALLOWED_LICENSES as a real permitted open-source license; retain the existing expression parser because tests prove it accepts unicode-ident's (MIT OR Apache-2.0) AND Unicode-3.0 expression only when every required AND term is permitted.
decisions.rationale: The owner resolved Q-0001 after identifying Unicode-3.0 as an OSI-approved permissive license, approved in November 2023, used transitively by the ICU4X family and unicode-ident. The policy change recognizes that license directly rather than waiving, suppressing, or bypassing dependency checks.

testEvidence.test: The focused license suite accepts Unicode-3.0, accepts (MIT OR Apache-2.0) AND Unicode-3.0, rejects an allowed OR group combined by AND with LicenseRef-Unknown, and removes a Unicode-3.0 package from the disallowed-package result.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: exit 1. Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 2 failed | 2 passed (4); Duration 427ms. Unicode-3.0 returned false where true was expected, and findDisallowedPackages still returned tinystr plus mystery instead of only mystery.

green.outcome: GREEN: exit 0. Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 4 passed (4); Duration 490ms. The allowed Unicode and unicode-ident expressions passed, while the unknown required AND term remained rejected.

testEvidence.test: Run the exact Rust-only and combined npm-plus-Rust license commands to zero exit and retain their complete merged stdout/stderr output outside the product repository.

red.command: pnpm.cmd check:licenses rust; pnpm.cmd check:licenses all
red.outcome: BASELINE POLICY RED: each command exited 1. Rust reported exactly 19 rejected records: 18 Unicode-3.0 package records plus unicode-ident@1.0.24 as (MIT OR Apache-2.0) AND Unicode-3.0. The combined command first passed npm for 117 packages, then failed on the same 19 Rust records.

green.command: cmd.exe /d /s /c "pnpm.cmd check:licenses rust 2>&1"; cmd.exe /d /s /c "pnpm.cmd check:licenses all 2>&1"
green.outcome: GREEN: both commands exited 0. Rust license policy passed: 478 packages checked. Combined output: npm license policy passed: 117 packages checked; Rust license policy passed: 478 packages checked. Exact output is retained at ../gate-scadmill-2026-07-10/artifacts/q-0001-unicode-license-20260711T191320Z; rust output SHA-256 CFEB497D84A79BCBE6FA81AD8D470C24C320636EF8374B71B05F8DE4FCCAD185 and all output SHA-256 52B88F99EF96FE851D01B33690C27E80F93782254E5E599383EAEFE5053696C8.

questions: Q-0001 is resolved by the owner's explicit Unicode-3.0 allow-list decision; no dependency-license policy question remains for these ICU4X and unicode-ident records.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
